mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.39% represents near-zero policy rates in an emergency easing cycle. Sustained low FEDFUNDS signals continued monetary accommodation, which suppresses long-term Treasury yields through expectations of prolonged low short rates and reduced inflation risk premium. Treasury yields decline as the market prices in extended monetary support.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% as of 2008-11-15 indicates emergency policy rate near zero lower bound
regime_hint: monetary emergency easing cycle post-financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises on average over 20-day forward windows when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window during 2008-2010 crisis period.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.7% reflects labor market deterioration during the financial crisis. Rising unemployment signals persistent economic weakness and deflationary pressure, causing investors to shift portfolio allocation toward safe-haven long-duration assets. This flight-to-quality demand compresses Treasury yields downward as investors accept lower yields for safety.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% as of 2008-11-15 indicates elevated unemployment during crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield fails to decline when UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, or correlation between monthly UNRATE changes and 20-day Treasury yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over 2008-2009 period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 355,655 million dollars show contraction relative to pre-crisis trend. Declining consumer demand signals recession deepening, reducing inflation expectations and causing the market to price in lower future short rates. Lower inflation expectations compress the term premium embedded in long-term Treasury yields, driving yields downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355,655M as of 2008-11-15 reflects demand collapse during financial crisis
regime_hint: demand-driven recession with collapsing consumer spending
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when RSAFS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day Treasury yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over 2008-2009.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 136,167 thousand shows ongoing job losses during the crisis. Declining PAYEMS signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which reduces aggregate demand expectations and increases the probability of deflation. Deflation expectations cause real yields to rise relative to nominal yields, but nominal Treasury yields compress downward as investors demand safety and lower real rates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines more than 500 thousand month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136,167K as of 2008-11-15 reflects severe employment losses
regime_hint: severe labor market contraction in financial crisis
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when PAYEMS declines by more than 500K month-over-month, or correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day Treasury yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over crisis period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 106.12 shows supply-side contraction. Declining INDPRO signals reduced economic capacity utilization and deflationary pressure from weak demand relative to supply. This supply-demand imbalance reduces inflation expectations, compressing the inflation risk premium in Treasury yields and driving nominal yields downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.12 as of 2008-11-15 reflects industrial contraction
regime_hint: supply-side recession with falling capacity utilization
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when INDPRO declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window during 2008-2010.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 791 thousand represent a severe contraction from pre-crisis levels. Collapsing HOUST signals both immediate demand destruction and future deflationary pressure from excess housing supply. The combination of falling demand and rising supply overhang compresses long-term inflation expectations, causing Treasury yields to fall as investors reprice inflation risk downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 791K as of 2008-11-15 reflects housing market collapse
regime_hint: housing-led demand collapse with deflationary pressure
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when HOUST is below 800K, or correlation between HOUST and 20-day Treasury yield changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over 2008-2009.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of near-zero FEDFUNDS (0.39%) with elevated UNRATE (6.7%) signals a regime where monetary policy is exhausted and labor market weakness dominates economic outlook. When policy rates are at the zero lower bound and unemployment is rising, the market expects prolonged economic slack and deflationary pressure. This joint condition compresses Treasury yields as investors price in extended low-rate environment and reduced inflation risk.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% and UNRATE at 6.7% as of 2008-11-15
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with rising unemployment
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when both FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and UNRATE > 6.5%, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either signal alone over 2008-2009 period.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of collapsing RSAFS (355,655M) with depressed HOUST (791K) signals a regime of synchronized demand destruction across consumer and housing sectors. When both retail sales and housing starts fall simultaneously, the market reprices long-term growth expectations downward and reduces inflation risk premium. This dual demand shock compresses Treasury yields as investors shift to safe-haven duration and price in extended period of below-trend growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 850 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355,655M and HOUST at 791K as of 2008-11-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand collapse across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises when both RSAFS declines 2% and HOUST < 850K, or the interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than either signal alone over 2008-2009 period.
